item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No vote from me. The early references to nexus corresponding to abstract ideas and influencing reality to that effect was interesting and promising, but in this case it didn't really go anywhere for me.

The last sections seem fairly disjointed, is there some reference in the ritual at the blacksmith shop or in the 12 people?

Btw, references to blacksmith should be changed to either blacksmith shop, or smithy. For a while there, I was trying to figure out how 12 people and an anvil fit inside a dude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because a ghost chasing show got lost here, I would love to see an alternate Parawatch Wiki view of this anomaly. I think the feel of the anomaly fits really well with their aesthetic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is wonderfully eerie. Echoing barredowl regarding negative space, imo, this is one of the best uses of it I've seen not just on the wiki but in general. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved this in the draft stage, love it now. This article manages to do a lot with negative space, and the tone and mood set by this article is really evocative and subtly creepy. While I am generally resistant to [DATA EXPUNGED] and the like, I think you pulled it off quite well here. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor nitpick*

The tendency for groups of five or more individuals to lose at least one member while inside SCP-5352; in most cases, this is simply a temporary separation.

From this line it seems, to me, as if an obvious part of the containment procedure would be "no groups of larger than 4 members are permitted to enter the area". Instead with the 5 man teams it's like they're specifically asking for researchers and agents to potentially get disappeared.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: novoting; this seems like a middle of the road 5000 contest "mystery" entry that lacks any punch or closure (including the last paragraph). waiting to see the tags if that elucidates anything for me, but i'm not really feeling this one for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This doesn't really come alive until the last paragraph, some entries on the list are nice and everything works well as setup. But you can only build up for so long, and a piece like this demands a lot more character than it currently has to meet its full potential. William Plunkett's fate could've been a through-line to build an article around but instead it's a very well-done but far too brief ending which only made me go back and realize how much better this SCP could be.
